sourceTrail.title: Confirmation Bias: A Ubiquitous Phenomenon in Many Guises
sourceTrail.note: A broad review that helps distinguish selective search, selective weighting, and memory distortion.

sourceTrail.title: The Case for Motivated Reasoning
sourceTrail.note: A foundational statement of how directional goals can bend the evaluation of evidence.

sourceTrail.title: Availability: A Heuristic for Judging Frequency and Probability
sourceTrail.note: The original paper introducing availability as a shortcut for probability and frequency judgment.

sourceTrail.title: Judgment under Uncertainty: Heuristics and Biases
sourceTrail.note: The classic heuristics paper that includes the anchoring-and-adjustment idea in its canonical form.

sourceTrail.title: Judgment under Uncertainty: Heuristics and Biases
sourceTrail.note: A strong starting point for the larger heuristics program that made base-rate neglect a core teaching case.

sourceTrail.title: The Trouble with Overconfidence
sourceTrail.note: Useful for separating overestimation, overplacement, and overprecision instead of treating overconfidence as a single thing.

sourceTrail.title: Unskilled and Unaware of It: How Difficulties in Recognizing One's Own Incompetence Lead to Inflated Self-Assessments
sourceTrail.source: Journal of Personality and Social Psychology
sourceTrail.note: The flagship paper behind the now-famous competence-and-self-assessment effect.

sourceTrail.title: Prospect Theory: An Analysis of Decision under Risk
sourceTrail.note: The canonical source for the asymmetric weighting of gains and losses in risky choice.

sourceTrail.title: The Framing of Decisions and the Psychology of Choice
sourceTrail.note: The standard source for preference reversals caused by equivalent but differently described options.

sourceTrail.title: Victims of Groupthink
sourceTrail.note: Janis's original formulation of the concept, its symptoms, and the policy fiasco examples that made it durable.

sourceTrail.title: Exploring the Planning Fallacy: Why People Underestimate Their Task Completion Times
sourceTrail.source: Journal of Personality and Social Psychology
sourceTrail.note: The best single starting point for optimistic time prediction and inside-view planning errors.

sourceTrail.title: The Psychology of Sunk Cost
sourceTrail.source: Organizational Behavior and Human Decision Processes
sourceTrail.note: The classic experiments on staying committed because previous investment feels too painful to abandon.

sourceTrail.title: Status Quo Bias in Decision Making
sourceTrail.note: The flagship demonstrations of how inherited options gain extra pull simply by already being in place.

sourceTrail.title: Do Defaults Save Lives?
sourceTrail.note: A widely taught demonstration of how preselection can quietly steer consequential decisions.

sourceTrail.title: The Intuitive Psychologist and His Shortcomings: Distortions in the Attribution Process
sourceTrail.note: The chapter most commonly cited as the conceptual home of the fundamental attribution error.

sourceTrail.title: A Constant Error in Psychological Ratings
sourceTrail.note: Thorndike's original paper naming the halo effect and showing cross-trait spillover in ratings.

sourceTrail.title: Implicit Social Cognition: Attitudes, Self-Esteem, and Stereotypes
sourceTrail.note: The landmark paper that helped define implicit social cognition as a research program.

sourceTrail.title: Implicit Social Cognition
sourceTrail.note: A modern review that is useful for scope, methods, and caution around overclaiming what implicit measures can show.

sourceTrail.title: Bad Is Stronger Than Good
sourceTrail.note: A widely cited review on why negative events and cues so often dominate attention, learning, and memory.

sourceTrail.title: Naive Realism in Everyday Life: Implications for Social Conflict and Misunderstanding
sourceTrail.note: The clearest source for the social-psychological version of naive realism used in conflict and disagreement work.

sourceTrail.title: Hindsight Does Not Equal Foresight: The Effect of Outcome Knowledge on Judgment under Uncertainty
sourceTrail.source: Journal of Experimental Psychology: Human Perception and Performance
sourceTrail.note: The original outcome-knowledge paper behind the hindsight effect.

sourceTrail.title: Survivorship Bias and Mutual Fund Performance
sourceTrail.note: A concrete domain example of how winner-only samples can make a process look much stronger than it really was.

sourceTrail.title: The Bias Blind Spot: Perceptions of Bias in Self Versus Others
sourceTrail.note: The defining paper on why people spot bias more readily in others than in themselves.

sourceTrail.title: Extensional Versus Intuitive Reasoning: The Conjunction Fallacy in Probability Judgment
sourceTrail.note: The classic Linda-style demonstrations that made the conjunction fallacy central to judgment research.

sourceTrail.title: Misinformation and Its Correction: Continued Influence and Successful Debiasing
sourceTrail.source: Psychological Science in the Public Interest
sourceTrail.note: A strong review of why retracted information keeps shaping later reasoning and what kinds of corrections help.

sourceTrail.title: The Continued Influence of Misinformation in Memory: What Makes a Correction Effective?
sourceTrail.source: The Psychology of Learning and Motivation
sourceTrail.note: A useful bridge from early experiments to later work on correction quality and explanation replacement.

sourceTrail.title: Action Bias Among Elite Soccer Goalkeepers: The Case of Penalty Kicks
sourceTrail.note: A memorable applied demonstration that visible action can become more attractive than statistically defensible restraint.

sourceTrail.title: Risk, Ambiguity, and the Savage Axioms
sourceTrail.note: Ellsberg's classic paradox is the clean starting point for ambiguity aversion and known-risk preference.

sourceTrail.title: Maps of Bounded Rationality: Psychology for Behavioral Economics
sourceTrail.note: A compact source for the idea that people often answer an easier question in place of the harder one actually asked.

sourceTrail.title: Behavioral Study of Obedience
sourceTrail.source: Journal of Abnormal and Social Psychology
sourceTrail.note: Milgram's obedience work is a strong anchor for the social power of perceived authority, even when the page frames the issue as judgment rather than obedience alone.

sourceTrail.title: Automation Bias: Decision Making and Performance in High-Tech Cockpits
sourceTrail.note: A central applied source for omission and commission errors when operators over-trust automated decision aids.

sourceTrail.title: Availability Cascades and Risk Regulation
sourceTrail.note: The defining source for how public repetition, social uptake, and risk perception can reinforce one another.

sourceTrail.title: Bandwagon, Snob, and Veblen Effects in the Theory of Consumers' Demand
sourceTrail.note: A classic source for preferences that change because other people are visibly choosing or valuing something.

sourceTrail.title: On the Conflict Between Logic and Belief in Syllogistic Reasoning
sourceTrail.note: The standard experimental source for how believable conclusions can distort validity judgments.

sourceTrail.title: Misremembrance of Options Past: Source Monitoring and Choice
sourceTrail.note: A useful source for how memory can become friendlier to the option one selected after the choice is made.

sourceTrail.title: Cognitive Consequences of Forced Compliance
sourceTrail.source: Journal of Abnormal and Social Psychology
sourceTrail.note: The classic forced-compliance experiment behind many modern lessons about attitude change after inconsistency.

sourceTrail.title: Compassion Fade: Affect and Charity Are Greatest for a Single Child in Need
sourceTrail.note: A direct empirical source for the way affective response can weaken as the number of victims grows.

sourceTrail.title: The Curse of Knowledge in Economic Settings: An Experimental Analysis
sourceTrail.note: A defining source for why people who know an answer struggle to model what it is like not to know it.

sourceTrail.title: Adding Asymmetrically Dominated Alternatives: Violations of Regularity and the Similarity Hypothesis
sourceTrail.note: The canonical source for decoy options that change preference between the main alternatives.

sourceTrail.title: Experimental Tests of the Endowment Effect and the Coase Theorem
sourceTrail.note: A central source for ownership-driven valuation gaps in ordinary market-like exchanges.

sourceTrail.title: The False Consensus Effect: An Egocentric Bias in Social Perception and Attribution Processes
sourceTrail.note: The defining paper for overestimating how widely one's own choices and views are shared.

sourceTrail.title: Social Cognition and Children's Aggressive Behavior
sourceTrail.note: A foundational source for hostile interpretation patterns in ambiguous social situations.

sourceTrail.title: The Misunderstood Limits of Folk Science: An Illusion of Explanatory Depth
sourceTrail.note: The core source for why people can feel they understand mechanisms until asked to explain them in detail.

sourceTrail.title: Social Categorization and Intergroup Behaviour
sourceTrail.note: A classic minimal-group source for how thin group boundaries can still shape allocation and evaluation.

sourceTrail.title: Observer's Reaction to the Innocent Victim: Compassion or Rejection?
sourceTrail.source: Journal of Personality and Social Psychology
sourceTrail.note: A classic source for the uncomfortable route from seeing innocent suffering to derogating the victim.

sourceTrail.title: Attitudinal Effects of Mere Exposure
sourceTrail.source: Journal of Personality and Social Psychology
sourceTrail.note: Zajonc's original paper showing how repeated exposure can increase liking without new evidence.

sourceTrail.title: Probability Neglect: Emotions, Worst Cases, and Law
sourceTrail.note: A useful source for cases where emotionally vivid outcomes overwhelm probability-sensitive judgment.

sourceTrail.title: Warnings During Disaster: Normalizing Communicated Risk
sourceTrail.note: A strong disaster-warning source for how people normalize risk information before acting on it.

sourceTrail.title: Reluctance to Vaccinate: Omission Bias and Ambiguity
sourceTrail.note: A classic applied source for treating harmful inaction as psychologically cleaner than harmful action.

sourceTrail.title: Unrealistic Optimism About Future Life Events
sourceTrail.source: Journal of Personality and Social Psychology
sourceTrail.note: A central source for the belief that one's own future risk is better than comparable others' risk.

sourceTrail.title: Outcome Bias in Decision Evaluation
sourceTrail.source: Journal of Personality and Social Psychology
sourceTrail.note: The key source for evaluating a decision differently because its result is already known.

sourceTrail.title: Doing It Now or Later
sourceTrail.note: A useful economic model for immediate-cost and delayed-benefit distortions in planning and self-control.

sourceTrail.title: Projection Bias in Predicting Future Utility
sourceTrail.note: The main source for how current states get over-projected into predictions about future preference.

sourceTrail.title: A Theory of Psychological Reactance
sourceTrail.note: Brehm's original formulation of the autonomy-threat response that makes restriction itself motivationally important.

sourceTrail.title: Self-Serving Biases in the Attribution of Causality: Fact or Fiction?
sourceTrail.note: A classic review source for asymmetric credit and blame patterns in causal attribution.

sourceTrail.title: A New Scale of Social Desirability Independent of Psychopathology
sourceTrail.note: The classic measurement source behind social-desirability response tendencies in self-report.

sourceTrail.title: The Spotlight Effect in Social Judgment: An Egocentric Bias in Estimates of the Salience of One's Own Actions and Appearance
sourceTrail.source: Journal of Personality and Social Psychology
sourceTrail.note: The flagship source for overestimating how much others notice one's appearance, behavior, or mistakes.
